outlook.headline: AEC demand remains broadening, though mention momentum is easing
outlook.summary: Active electrical cables still look to be in an improving phase over the next 1-2 quarters: every covered name was bullish, and the strongest reads point to hot demand, broadening adoption, and multiple design wins. The softer mention trend (0.3x, -5% vs trailing average) suggests the story is not accelerating from here, but it is still expanding from a narrow niche into a broader volume opportunity, led by Credo, Microchip, and Marvell, with Astera and MaxLinear also participating.

drivers.label: Broad adoption and volume shipments
drivers.detail: Evidence that AECs are moving beyond early deployment into sustained production demand.

drivers.label: Core growth engine / broadening use cases
drivers.detail: AEC is being described as a central growth driver with multiple new vectors, signaling expanding TAM.

drivers.label: Rapid market growth support
drivers.detail: Retimer demand tied to rapid AEC market growth points to continued shipment acceleration.

drivers.label: Multiple design wins and strong interest
drivers.detail: Healthy pipeline signals near-term conversion into revenue across deployments.

drivers.label: Scale-up participation but less emphatic tone
drivers.detail: AEC opportunity is still growing, though commentary is more measured than on the strongest hyperscale/1.6T themes.

risks: Mention trend is slipping modestly versus the trailing average, which could hint at a near-term pause in narrative momentum.
If deployment timing shifts, broad adoption could take longer to convert into shipment growth.
Any cooling in design-win conversion would disproportionately impact the more bullish growth cases at Credo and Marvell.
